channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Part of this massive pothole os 50cm wide and 7cm deep., 10th April
item.description:
Near to Hillsdown and Watermead Farm in the lane which is a single track no pavements. Pothole is usually filled with rain water but has now dried out due to weather to show how deep it is. Very dangerous to vehicle suspension when it is wet - due to no visibility of the depth of this pothole which is usually filled with rain water, and could be a potential trip hazard to pedestrians.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Double yellows worn off, 10th April
item.description:
Double yellows worn and almost invisible outside premier Regular dangerous parking, repainting will hopefully discourage this
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Merriville Road, Wymans Brook, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Road marking white lines or studs

item.title: The potholes in beeches road (opposite no 7) and at junction with little herberts road, 10th April
item.description:
These potholes have now become dangerously deep and almost unavoidable. Danger of collision with oncoming traffic. Last reported over a month ago and still awaiting attention.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Beeches Road, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish and overgrown brambles etc and now vermin, 10th April
item.description:
The area behind Welch road and back of lipson road the ex garages site is filled with fly tipping and so overgrown that there are now rats coming into my garden this is an ongoing problem that is only going to get worse with summer coming

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Lipson Road, Kingsditch, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree is damaged problem, 10th April
item.description:
Email received 08/04/2026 - copy attached. Customer reports that the Lime Tree at the location has caused the boundary wall to subside and consequently it has been taken down. The wall needs to be rebuilt but they are wary of doing so, as the tree could potentially cause the same issues for this as well.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This car has been parked in Glamorgan Road since before the Festival races meeting, ie; more than a month unattended., 10th April
item.description:
It would be good to know who the owner is and that hasn’t been abandoned. Large vehicles are having to go onto the opposite pavement to get past it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4, Glamorgan Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A pothole in road problem, 10th April
item.description:
Date of incident: 20 February 2026
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge pothole. Too big to drive around. I drove into yesterday and it was awful, 10th April
item.description:
Location on the entry. There are lots of potholes along this road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Potholes at entrance to Witcombe Farm, 10th April
item.description:
This entrance is used for the stables and potholes cause a danger to the horses and their riders at this point.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 10th April
item.description:
pothole CW 500x300x60mm Std TM Stats (CW6 Pothole)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street sign on side of house has been painted over (or possibly removed), 10th April
item.description:
On the wall of the house on the corner of Sussex Avenue and Rowanfield Road (number 3 I think). The house exterior was recently painted.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Sussex Avenue, Rowanfield, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Sign plate is missing (Non illuminating sign)

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 10th April
item.description:
Perm for ENWD pothole CW 600x500x85mm Std TM (CW6 Pothole)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 10th April
item.description:
ENWD pothole CW 600x500x85mm Std TM (CW6 Pothole)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bollard in the grass verge, not sure where it's from, 10th April
item.description:
Grass verge outside 179 Arle Road, just inside Brooklyn Gardens

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Brooklyn Gardens, Wymans Brook, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Complete bollard is damaged (Non illuminating bollard or post)

item.title: TMC - Defects - Other FW6 Surface problem, 10th April
item.description:
Abrupt Level Difference FW 250mm x 200mm x 25mm Give and take TM With Temporary FW Closure Stats required Fill around ironwork (FW6 Abrupt Level Differences)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trip hazard on pavement at one house up from Esso petrol station, 10th April
item.description:
Trip hazard on pavement at one house up from Esso petrol station There is a long width raised area of footway - possibly caused by a tree root. This is a significant trip hazard and should be repaired

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hucclecote Road, Hucclecote, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 10th April
item.description:
The red wood is from the rubbish dumped last weekend that wasn’t collected by the council when they “cleaned up the fly tipping” on Tuesday. The black netting arrived between 07:00 and 12:00 on Wednesday and was joined by the suitcase, mattress and black bags between 20:00 Wednesday and 07:00 on Thursday.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 66, Pates Avenue, Alstone, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 10th April
item.description:
Pothole CW 400x400x60mm approx Std TM (CW6 Pothole)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Footways Slabs problem, 10th April
item.description:
Broken slab. Please replace 600x770mm Standard TM with temp fw closure Stats (FW Broken Slabs)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Footways Slabs problem, 10th April
item.description:
Rocking unstable slab Please lift and relay 600x600mm Standard TM with temp FW Closure Stats (FW Rocking/Unstable Slab)
Report on FixMyStreet
